_schema: spec #28 Phase 0.3 — 10 reference questions for the O1 + O5 acceptance gate

_field_docs.id: stable identifier (q01..q10) — used as a test parametrization key
_field_docs.question: the natural-language question Claude is asked, verbatim
_field_docs.expected_entities: entities Claude should reference at least once (canonical id form, e.g. person:elon-musk)
_field_docs.expected_quote_fragments: substrings that must appear in at least one cited quote (string match, case-insensitive)
_field_docs.min_distinct_episodes: minimum number of distinct episode_ids cited in the answer (broad questions: 3+; narrow: 1)
_field_docs.category: what failure mode this question stresses; lets the runner group failures by class
_field_docs.notes: scratchpad for the human grader; ungraded by the runner

_targets.phase_1_gate: Run against the SQL-only MCP alpha. Pass = 10/10 no-fabrication, every quote traceable to a tool result in the same turn.
_targets.phase_2_gate: Re-run against the full hybrid surface (entity layer + qmd). Same pass criteria.
_targets.nightly: Run against fixture corpus; regressions block release.

questions.question: What has Scott Galloway said about the SpaceX IPO across recent Prof G Markets episodes, and how has his framing evolved?
questions.notes: Stresses cross-episode aggregation on a single host. Answer must be narrative + cited; the SpaceX IPO is covered in both Prof G Markets and the Prof G Pod, so getting the right host attribution matters.

questions.question: What did Greg Brockman say about GPT-5.5 'Spud' on Big Technology Podcast?
questions.notes: Tests whether find_mentions / list_quotes_by can pin a single guest interview without confusion with surrounding episodes about OpenAI.

questions.question: Across the corpus, who has discussed Tim Cook stepping down from Apple, and what reasons are given?
questions.notes: Tim Cook stepping down was covered by Big Tech Podcast (Joanna Stern interview), Prof G Markets, and discussed in passing on 20VC. Stresses cross-podcast aggregation.

questions.question: What has been said on Dwarkesh Podcast about bottlenecks to scaling AI compute?
questions.notes: Dylan Patel episode is the obvious hit but Ilya and Michael Nielsen episodes touch related themes — top-3 should include all three.

questions.question: Find every time guests on 20VC have discussed Cursor's acquisition by xAI for $60BN.
questions.notes: Lexical-friendly query (specific dollar figure). Hybrid mode should not lose this to semantic noise.

questions.question: What has been said across the corpus about the AI job crisis, unemployment from automation, and Andrew Yang's predictions?
questions.notes: Tests semantic recall (Prof G Markets explicit Andrew Yang ep + Lenny + Rest Is Money + 20VC). Should not just return the Andrew Yang ep.

questions.question: On The Rest Is History, what was said about the rise of Margaret Thatcher in the 1970s?
questions.notes: Anchors a non-AI question to make sure search isn't biased toward technology vocabulary. Two-part Britain in the 70s episodes.

questions.question: Across April 2026 episodes, what was said about Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 release?
questions.notes: Should hit Moonshots EP #248, AI Show #210, Lenny + 20VC. Tests both date_range filtering and entity-type=product.

questions.question: What have guests said about Iran in 2026 — in the context of the war and in the context of AI supply chain risk?
questions.notes: Same word, two completely different contexts (geopolitics vs. AI supply chain). Tests whether semantic mode can pull both clusters and present them as distinct.

questions.question: What have Geoffrey Hinton and Ilya Sutskever said about the future of AI research and where we go after pure scaling?
questions.notes: Tests boolean composition over speakers + topic. Hinton on StarTalk (Feb), Ilya on Dwarkesh (Nov). Should not return either in isolation.
